question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with a crafted startTime set to a past consensus timestamp of a fee-schedule update message, causing the service to re-deliver that fee-schedule message and trigger downstream fee recalculation outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 in TopicMessageFilter to bypass the hasLimit() guard and receive an unbounded stream of fee-related topic messages, exhausting downstream fee-processing resources and causing fee computation to deviate from design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with endTime set to exactly startTime+1 nanosecond to force the service to emit a single fee-schedule message in isolation, causing a partial fee update that violates the atomicity design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the safety-check gap-recovery path in missingMessages() by subscribing and immediately cancelling, causing the service to re-query and re-deliver fee-schedule update messages that were already applied, leading to double-application of fee changes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the numMissingMessages calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) by subscribing to a topic where sequence numbers have gaps around fee-schedule messages, causing the gap-fill query to retrieve and re-process fee updates outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the limit recalculation (filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get()) in incomingMessages() by rapidly subscribing and unsubscribing to cause the effective limit to underflow, resulting in an unbounded retrieval of fee-related messages that bypasses design-parameter limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId that resolves to a non-TOPIC entity type when checkTopicExists is false, causing the service to stream messages from a non-topic entity that contains fee-schedule data, bypassing the entity-type validation design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent subscriptions to a fee-schedule topic, incrementing subscriberCount to exhaust bounded-elastic scheduler threads, causing legitimate fee-update deliveries to be delayed or dropped outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value in the past (endTime < startTime of the context) to immediately mark the subscription as complete via isComplete(), causing the service to skip delivery of fee-schedule messages that fall within the intended window, effectively suppressing a fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an endTime that is exactly at the boundary of Instant.now() plus grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval(), causing isComplete() to oscillate between true and false and resulting in non-deterministic delivery of fee-schedule messages outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to the minimum allowed value (0) to retrieve all historical fee-schedule topic messages from genesis, causing the downstream fee processor to replay all historical fee changes and corrupt the current fee state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter validation in TopicMessageFilter (isValidStartTime checks startTime <= DomainUtils.now()) by submitting startTime equal to DomainUtils.now() at the exact nanosecond boundary, causing a race condition that allows retrieval of fee messages before they are fully committed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set a very large limit value (Long.MAX_VALUE) in TopicMessageFilter to cause the take(filter.getLimit()) operator to hold the stream open indefinitely, consuming all fee-schedule messages and preventing other subscribers from receiving timely fee updates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the missingMessages() function to construct a newFilter with endTime set to current.getConsensusTimestamp() of a fee-schedule message, and then exploit the concatWithValues(current) to deliver the fee message twice, causing double-processing of a fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic with a filter that has no endTime (null), causing the pastEndTime() to return Flux.never() and keeping the subscription alive indefinitely, monopolizing the topic listener and starving other fee-update consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the IllegalStateException in onNext() by crafting a scenario where out-of-order fee-schedule messages are delivered, causing the subscription to terminate abruptly and leaving the fee state in a partially-updated condition outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequence-number deduplication filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) to suppress delivery of a fee-schedule message by pre-seeding the last sequence number via a crafted prior subscription, causing a fee update to be silently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime recalculation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) in incomingMessages() by subscribing at a timestamp that is exactly 1 nanosecond before a fee-schedule message, causing the live listener to skip that message and deliver only subsequent fee updates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a subscriberId that collides with an existing active subscriber's ID (guessing the 8-character random string), causing the service to log and track fee-message delivery under the wrong subscriber context, corrupting audit trails for fee changes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) race condition to receive the same fee-schedule message from both the safetyCheck path and the live path simultaneously, causing the downstream processor to apply the same fee update twice?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missingMessages() to be invoked with current=null (safety check path) by deliberately stalling the live message stream, forcing a gap-recovery query that retrieves fee-schedule messages from a time window that has already been processed, leading to fee re-application?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to Long.MAX_VALUE in TopicMessageFilter to bypass the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) guard, causing the service to stream all future fee-schedule messages without any time-based termination?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isNext() check (topicMessage.getSequenceNumber() == getLast().getSequenceNumber() + 1) by subscribing to a topic where a fee-schedule message has sequence number that is exactly getLast()+1, bypassing gap detection and causing a fee update to be applied without verifying intermediate messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the historical Flux (topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(filter, true)) concatenated with the live Flux to receive fee-schedule messages that span a historical-to-live boundary, causing a fee update to be applied out of the intended chronological order?
